description_long: This is a mid-rise apartment building with a brick exterior, featuring five stories and a flat roof. The facade is adorned with regularly spaced rectangular windows, and prominent black metal fire escapes run along one side, providing emergency egress from each floor. The building's rear and side walls are less ornate, with some areas showing weathered or painted surfaces. The front of the building includes a main entrance at street level, and the windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical columns. The structure is typical of early-to-mid 20th-century urban residential architecture, often found in dense city neighborhoods. The overall appearance suggests durability and functionality, with utilitarian features like fire escapes and minimal decorative elements.
description: A five-story brick apartment building with symmetrical windows and black metal fire escapes on one side, typical of urban neighborhoods. The structure is sturdy and functional, with a flat roof and a main entrance at street level.
description_view_0: Side view showing the fire escapes and the depth of the building, with windows and a flat roof visible.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back wall, less ornate with some weathered surfaces and a partial view of balconies.
description_view_2: Oblique view showing both the fire escape side and the rear, with a clear view of window arrangements and building depth.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the main entrance, symmetrical window arrangement, and the full height of the fire escapes.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in evenly spaced vertical columns, creating a symmetrical and orderly pattern across each floor. Most windows are grouped in pairs or threes, aligned horizontally and vertically.
